title: Hassette Configuration
description: Configuration schema for hassette.toml — the Hassette automation framework config file.

database.description: Database storage, retention, and operational settings.

logging.description: Logging level, format, queue, and per-service log-level settings.

lifecycle.description: Startup, shutdown, and per-operation timeout settings.

web_api.description: Web API and UI server settings.

apps.description: App directory, auto-detection, and manifest settings.

blocking_io.description: Blocking-I/O detection settings for the shared event loop.

dev_mode.description: Enable developer mode, which may include additional logging and features.
dev_mode.title: Dev Mode

base_url.description: Base URL of the Home Assistant instance
base_url.title: Base Url

verify_ssl.description:
Whether to verify SSL certificates when connecting to Home Assistant. Useful to disable for self-signed
certificates.
verify_ssl.title: Verify Ssl

token.description:
Access token for Home Assistant instance.

Stored as a :class:`~pydantic.SecretStr` so the value is masked in logs
and string representations.  Unwrap with ``token.get_secret_value()`` when
the plaintext is required (e.g. HTTP auth headers, WebSocket auth payload).
token.title: Token

import_dot_env_files.description:
Whether to import .env files specified in env_files. With this disabled, the .env file provided will only
be used for loading settings. With this enabled, the .env files will also be loaded into os.environ.
import_dot_env_files.title: Import Dot Env Files

run_app_precheck.description:
Whether to run the app precheck before starting Hassette. This is recommended, but if any apps fail to load
then Hassette will not start.
run_app_precheck.title: Run App Precheck

allow_startup_if_app_precheck_fails.description: Whether to allow Hassette to start even if the app precheck fails. This is generally not recommended.
allow_startup_if_app_precheck_fails.title: Allow Startup If App Precheck Fails

hassette_event_buffer_size.description: Buffer capacity of the internal anyio memory channel used to route events to the bus.
hassette_event_buffer_size.title: Hassette Event Buffer Size

default_cache_size.description: Default size limit for caches in bytes. Defaults to 100 MiB.
default_cache_size.title: Default Cache Size

strict_lifecycle.description:
Enable strict validation for lifecycle transitions, connection state, and registries.

Controls three subsystems uniformly:
- Resource lifecycle: invalid ResourceStatus transitions raise InvalidLifecycleTransitionError
- WebSocket connection: invalid ConnectionState transitions raise InvalidLifecycleTransitionError
- Registry validation: startup issues raise RegistryValidationError

When False (default), all three subsystems log WARNING instead of raising.
The test harness sets this to True by default.
strict_lifecycle.title: Strict Lifecycle

asyncio_debug_mode.description: Whether to enable asyncio debug mode.
asyncio_debug_mode.title: Asyncio Debug Mode

state_proxy_poll_interval_seconds.description: Interval in seconds to poll the state proxy for updates.
state_proxy_poll_interval_seconds.title: State Proxy Poll Interval Seconds

disable_state_proxy_polling.description: Whether to disable polling for the state proxy. Defaults to False.
disable_state_proxy_polling.title: Disable State Proxy Polling

bus_excluded_domains.description: Domains whose events should be skipped by the bus; supports glob patterns (e.g. 'sensor', 'media_*').
bus_excluded_domains.title: Bus Excluded Domains

bus_excluded_entities.description: Entity IDs whose events should be skipped by the bus; supports glob patterns.
bus_excluded_entities.title: Bus Excluded Entities

allow_reload_in_prod.description:
Whether to enable the file watcher for automatic app reloads in production mode.

When True, file changes trigger automatic app reloads (same as dev_mode).
Manual app management (start/stop/reload via API) is always available
regardless of this setting. Defaults to False.
allow_reload_in_prod.title: Allow Reload In Prod

allow_only_app_in_prod.description: Whether to allow the `only_app` decorator in production mode. Defaults to False.
allow_only_app_in_prod.title: Allow Only App In Prod

forgotten_await_behavior.description:
Global default for forgotten-await detection behavior.

When ``None`` (default), the effective behavior is ``"warn"``.  Per-app
``AppConfig.forgotten_await_behavior`` overrides this when set.  Set to ``"ignore"``
to suppress warnings globally, or ``"error"`` to escalate via ``filterwarnings("error")``.

app_key.description: Reflects the key for this app in hassette.toml
app_key.title: App Key

enabled.description: Whether the app is enabled or not, will default to True if not set. Does not consider @only_app decorator.
enabled.title: Enabled

autostart.description:
Whether the app starts automatically when Hassette starts. Orthogonal to
`enabled`: an enabled app with autostart=false is registered and startable on
demand, but is not started at startup or by a live config reload.
autostart.title: Autostart

filename.description: Filename of the app, will be looked for in app_path
filename.title: Filename

display_name.description: Display name of the app, will use class_name if not set
display_name.title: Display Name

app_dir.description: Path to the app directory, relative to current working directory or absolute
app_dir.title: App Dir

auto_loaded.description: Whether the app was auto-detected or manually configured
auto_loaded.title: Auto Loaded

full_path.description: Fully resolved path to the app file
full_path.title: Full Path

AppsConfig.description:
App directory, auto-detection, exclusion, manifest, and raw-app-dict settings.

In TOML, app definitions live alongside these settings under ``[hassette.apps]``:

.. code-block:: toml

    [hassette.apps]
    directory = "apps"

    [hassette.apps.my_app]
    filename = "my_app.py"
    class_name = "MyApp"

The ``model_validator`` separates known config fields from app-definition
dicts so both coexist in the same TOML section.

autodetect.description: Whether to automatically detect apps in the app directory.
autodetect.title: Autodetect

extend_exclude_dirs.description: Additional directories to exclude when auto-detecting apps in the app directory.
extend_exclude_dirs.title: Extend Exclude Dirs

exclude_dirs.description:
Directories to exclude when auto-detecting apps. Prefer extend_exclude_dirs to avoid
removing the defaults.
exclude_dirs.title: Exclude Dirs

manifests.description: Validated app manifests, keyed by app name.
manifests.title: Manifests

apps.description: Raw configuration for Hassette apps, keyed by app name.
apps.title: Apps

directory.description: Directory to load user apps from.
directory.title: Directory

AppsConfig.title: AppsConfig

BlockingIOBehavior.description: Controls what happens when blocking I/O is detected on the shared event loop.
BlockingIOBehavior.title: BlockingIOBehavior

BlockingIODetectionConfig.description:
Blocking-I/O detection settings for the shared event loop.

Controls the two-tier detection system: Tier 1 (always-on responsiveness watchdog) and
Tier 2 (call-site interception of known blocking primitives, dev-default / prod-opt-in).

behavior.description:
Global default behavior for blocking-IO detection.

When ``None`` (default), the effective behavior is ``"warn"``. Per-app
``AppConfig.blocking_io_behavior`` overrides this when set. Set to ``"ignore"`` to
suppress detection globally, or ``"error"`` to escalate via ``filterwarnings("error")``.

watchdog_enabled.description: Whether to run the Tier 1 loop-responsiveness watchdog. Defaults to True.
watchdog_enabled.title: Watchdog Enabled

lag_threshold_seconds.description:
Tier 1: minimum loop lag in seconds before a blocking-detected event is emitted.
Defaults to 100ms — brief stalls under this threshold are not reported.
lag_threshold_seconds.title: Lag Threshold Seconds

watchdog_interval_seconds.description:
Tier 1: interval in seconds between watchdog heartbeat checks.
Defaults to 250ms — trades detection latency for overhead.
watchdog_interval_seconds.title: Watchdog Interval Seconds

capture_stack_on_block.description:
Whether to capture a stack snapshot of the loop thread when a severe stall is detected.
Defaults to True. Set to False to reduce overhead on low-memory systems.
capture_stack_on_block.title: Capture Stack On Block

deep_detection_enabled.description:
Whether to enable Tier 2 call-site interception (monkeypatching of blocking primitives).

When ``None`` (default), follows ``dev_mode``: enabled in dev, disabled in production.
Set explicitly to ``True`` or ``False`` to override the dev_mode default.
deep_detection_enabled.title: Deep Detection Enabled

allow_deep_detection_in_prod.description:
Whether to enable Tier 2 deep detection in production mode.

When True, Tier 2 call-site interception is active even when ``dev_mode`` is False,
regardless of ``deep_detection_enabled``. Mirrors ``allow_reload_in_prod`` semantics.
Defaults to False.
allow_deep_detection_in_prod.title: Allow Deep Detection In Prod

BlockingIODetectionConfig.title: BlockingIODetectionConfig

DatabaseConfig.description: Database storage, retention, write-queue, and operational-interval settings.

path.description: Path to the SQLite database file. Defaults to data_dir / "hassette.db" when None.
path.title: Path

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain execution records in the ``executions`` table.
retention_days.title: Retention Days

max_size_mb.description:
Maximum database file size in MB. When exceeded, oldest execution records are deleted.
0 disables the size failsafe.
max_size_mb.title: Max Size Mb

migration_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for SQL schema migrations to complete at startup.
migration_timeout_seconds.title: Migration Timeout Seconds

write_queue_max.description:
Maximum pending coroutines in the DatabaseService write queue. Bounds memory growth
under sustained I/O pressure. Fire-and-forget tasks are dropped on overflow; submit()
callers block until space is available.
write_queue_max.title: Write Queue Max

telemetry_write_queue_max.description: Maximum pending records in the CommandExecutor write queue before records are dropped.
telemetry_write_queue_max.title: Telemetry Write Queue Max

heartbeat_interval_seconds.description: Interval in seconds between database heartbeat checks.
heartbeat_interval_seconds.title: Heartbeat Interval Seconds

retention_interval_seconds.description: Interval in seconds between retention enforcement runs.
retention_interval_seconds.title: Retention Interval Seconds

size_failsafe_interval_seconds.description: Interval in seconds between size failsafe enforcement runs.
size_failsafe_interval_seconds.title: Size Failsafe Interval Seconds

size_failsafe_max_iterations.description: Maximum number of delete-batch iterations per size failsafe run.
size_failsafe_max_iterations.title: Size Failsafe Max Iterations

size_failsafe_delete_batch.description: Number of rows deleted per batch during size failsafe enforcement.
size_failsafe_delete_batch.title: Size Failsafe Delete Batch

size_failsafe_vacuum_pages.description: Number of pages to vacuum per size failsafe run.
size_failsafe_vacuum_pages.title: Size Failsafe Vacuum Pages

max_consecutive_heartbeat_failures.description: Maximum consecutive heartbeat failures before the database service is considered unhealthy.
max_consecutive_heartbeat_failures.title: Max Consecutive Heartbeat Failures

read_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for a telemetry read query before raising TimeoutError.
read_timeout_seconds.title: Read Timeout Seconds

max_flush_interval_seconds.description:
Maximum seconds a record may sit in the CommandExecutor write queue before a
time-based flush is forced, even if the batch size threshold has not been reached.
max_flush_interval_seconds.title: Max Flush Interval Seconds

DatabaseConfig.title: DatabaseConfig

FileWatcherConfig.description: File watcher debounce, step, and enable/disable settings.

debounce_milliseconds.description: Debounce time for file watcher events in milliseconds.
debounce_milliseconds.title: Debounce Milliseconds

step_milliseconds.description: Time to wait for additional file changes before emitting event in milliseconds.
step_milliseconds.title: Step Milliseconds

watch_files.description: Whether to watch files for changes and reload apps automatically.
watch_files.title: Watch Files

FileWatcherConfig.title: FileWatcherConfig

ForgottenAwaitBehavior.description: Controls what happens when a protected method is called without ``await``.
ForgottenAwaitBehavior.title: ForgottenAwaitBehavior

LifecycleConfig.description: Startup, shutdown, and per-operation timeout settings for the resource lifecycle.

startup_timeout_seconds.description:
Length of time to wait for each wave of Hassette resources to start before giving up.
Must be >= app_startup_timeout_seconds since AppHandler readiness waits for app bootstrap.
startup_timeout_seconds.title: Startup Timeout Seconds

app_startup_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for an app to start before giving up.
app_startup_timeout_seconds.title: App Startup Timeout Seconds

app_shutdown_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for an app to shut down before giving up.
app_shutdown_timeout_seconds.title: App Shutdown Timeout Seconds

resource_shutdown_timeout_seconds.description: Per-phase timeout for resource shutdown. Defaults to app_shutdown_timeout_seconds.
resource_shutdown_timeout_seconds.title: Resource Shutdown Timeout Seconds

total_shutdown_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum wall-clock seconds for the entire Hassette shutdown (hooks + propagation).
total_shutdown_timeout_seconds.title: Total Shutdown Timeout Seconds

sync_executor_max_workers.description:
Pool ceiling for the dedicated sync-handler thread pool executor.
Sized to match the prior implicit default-pool ceiling (min(32, cpu+4)). This is a
reasonable starting ceiling, NOT a literal behavior-equivalence guarantee: the old
shared pool also served logging and DB work, so a same-size dedicated pool gives sync
handlers more effective headroom in practice.
sync_executor_max_workers.title: Sync Executor Max Workers

sync_executor_shutdown_timeout_seconds.description:
Join-or-interrupt budget for sync-handler worker threads at shutdown (ported from HA).
Must be less than total_shutdown_timeout_seconds so the outer shutdown budget is not
exceeded by the executor interrupt phase.
sync_executor_shutdown_timeout_seconds.title: Sync Executor Shutdown Timeout Seconds

registration_await_timeout.description:
Timeout in seconds to wait for all pending listener/job DB registrations to flush
before post-ready reconciliation. Prevents indefinite hangs if the DB write queue stalls.
registration_await_timeout.title: Registration Await Timeout

event_handler_timeout_seconds.description:
Default timeout in seconds for event handler execution. ``None`` disables the default timeout.
Individual listeners can override via ``timeout=`` or ``timeout_disabled=True``.
event_handler_timeout_seconds.title: Event Handler Timeout Seconds

max_concurrent_dispatches.description:
Ceiling on concurrent event-handler invocations dispatched by the bus.

The bus fans an event out to one task per matching listener. Without a bound, a state
storm hitting many listeners spawns unbounded tasks and can exhaust memory and the event
loop. When this many handlers are already in flight, the bus waits for a slot before
spawning more — that wait propagates back through the inbound event channel to the
Home Assistant WebSocket reader (true backpressure).

A running handler holds its slot until it returns or hits ``event_handler_timeout_seconds``,
so a slow handler can stall dispatch up to that timeout. Read once at startup; changing it
requires a restart.
max_concurrent_dispatches.title: Max Concurrent Dispatches

error_handler_timeout_seconds.description: Default timeout in seconds for error handler execution. ``None`` disables the default timeout.
error_handler_timeout_seconds.title: Error Handler Timeout Seconds

run_sync_timeout_seconds.description: Default timeout for synchronous function calls.
run_sync_timeout_seconds.title: Run Sync Timeout Seconds

task_cancellation_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for tasks to cancel before forcing.
task_cancellation_timeout_seconds.title: Task Cancellation Timeout Seconds

LifecycleConfig.title: LifecycleConfig

LoggingConfig.description: Logging level, format, queue, persistence, and per-service log-level settings.

log_format.description:
Console output format. ``"auto"`` detects TTY vs pipe automatically. ``"console"`` forces
colored human-readable output. ``"json"`` forces one-JSON-object-per-line output.
log_format.title: Log Format

log_queue_max.description: Maximum size of the inter-thread log queue. Records are dropped when the queue is full.
log_queue_max.title: Log Queue Max

log_persistence_level.description: Minimum log level for database persistence. Records below this level are not stored.
log_persistence_level.title: Log Persistence Level

log_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain persisted log records. Must be <= database.retention_days.
log_retention_days.title: Log Retention Days

database_service.description: Logging level for the database service. Defaults to log_level.
database_service.title: Database Service

bus_service.description: Logging level for the event bus service. Defaults to log_level.
bus_service.title: Bus Service

scheduler_service.description: Logging level for the scheduler service. Defaults to log_level.
scheduler_service.title: Scheduler Service

app_handler.description: Logging level for the app handler service. Defaults to log_level.
app_handler.title: App Handler

web_api.description: Logging level for the web API service. Defaults to log_level.
web_api.title: Web Api

websocket.description: Logging level for the WebSocket service. Defaults to log_level.
websocket.title: Websocket

service_watcher.description: Logging level for the service watcher. Defaults to log_level.
service_watcher.title: Service Watcher

file_watcher.description: Logging level for the file watcher service. Defaults to log_level.
file_watcher.title: File Watcher

task_bucket.description: Logging level for task buckets. Defaults to log_level.
task_bucket.title: Task Bucket

command_executor.description: Logging level for the command executor service. Defaults to log_level.
command_executor.title: Command Executor

apps.description: Default logging level for apps, can be overridden in app initialization. Defaults to log_level.
apps.title: Apps

state_proxy.description: Logging level for the state proxy resource. Defaults to log_level.
state_proxy.title: State Proxy

api.description: Logging level for the API resource (REST/WebSocket client). Defaults to log_level.
api.title: Api

all_events.description: Whether to include all events in bus debug logging. Should be used sparingly.
all_events.title: All Events

all_hass_events.description:
Whether to include all Home Assistant events in bus debug logging.
Defaults to False or the value of all_events.
all_hass_events.title: All Hass Events

all_hassette_events.description:
Whether to include all Hassette events in bus debug logging.
Defaults to False or the value of all_events.
all_hassette_events.title: All Hassette Events

LoggingConfig.title: LoggingConfig

RawAppDict.description:
Structure for raw app configuration before processing.

Not all fields are required at this stage, as we will enrich and validate them later.
RawAppDict.title: RawAppDict

SchedulerConfig.description: Scheduler delay, threshold, and job-timeout settings.

behind_schedule_threshold_seconds.description: Threshold in seconds before a 'behind schedule' warning is logged for a job.
behind_schedule_threshold_seconds.title: Behind Schedule Threshold Seconds

job_timeout_seconds.description:
Default timeout in seconds for scheduled job execution. ``None`` disables the default timeout.
Individual jobs can override via ``timeout=`` or ``timeout_disabled=True``.
job_timeout_seconds.title: Job Timeout Seconds

SchedulerConfig.title: SchedulerConfig

WebApiConfig.description: Web API and UI server host, port, buffer, and feature-flag settings.

run.description: Whether to run the web API service (includes healthcheck and UI backend).
run.title: Run

run_ui.description: Whether to serve the web UI dashboard. Only used when run is True.
run_ui.title: Run Ui

ui_hot_reload.description: Watch web UI static files and templates for changes and push live reloads to the browser.
ui_hot_reload.title: Ui Hot Reload

host.description: Host to bind the web API server to.
host.title: Host

port.description: Port to run the web API server on.
port.title: Port

cors_origins.description: Allowed CORS origins for the web API, typically the UI dev server.
cors_origins.title: Cors Origins

event_buffer_size.description: Maximum number of recent events to keep in the RuntimeQueryService ring buffer.
event_buffer_size.title: Event Buffer Size

log_buffer_size.description: Maximum number of log entries to keep in the LogCaptureHandler ring buffer.
log_buffer_size.title: Log Buffer Size

job_history_size.description: Maximum number of job execution records to keep.
job_history_size.title: Job History Size

WebApiConfig.title: WebApiConfig

WebSocketConfig.description: WebSocket connection, retry, and recovery timing settings.

authentication_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for WebSocket authentication to complete.
authentication_timeout_seconds.title: Authentication Timeout Seconds

response_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for a response from the WebSocket.
response_timeout_seconds.title: Response Timeout Seconds

connection_timeout_seconds.description: Length of time to wait for WebSocket connection to complete. Passed to aiohttp.
connection_timeout_seconds.title: Connection Timeout Seconds

total_timeout_seconds.description: Total length of time to wait for WebSocket operations to complete. Passed to aiohttp.
total_timeout_seconds.title: Total Timeout Seconds

heartbeat_interval_seconds.description: Interval to send ping messages to keep the WebSocket connection alive. Passed to aiohttp.
heartbeat_interval_seconds.title: Heartbeat Interval Seconds

connect_retry_max_attempts.description: Maximum number of attempts to establish the initial WebSocket connection before giving up.
connect_retry_max_attempts.title: Connect Retry Max Attempts

connect_retry_initial_wait_seconds.description: Initial backoff wait in seconds between WebSocket connection retry attempts.
connect_retry_initial_wait_seconds.title: Connect Retry Initial Wait Seconds

connect_retry_max_wait_seconds.description: Maximum backoff wait in seconds between WebSocket connection retry attempts.
connect_retry_max_wait_seconds.title: Connect Retry Max Wait Seconds

early_drop_stable_window_seconds.description: Seconds a connection must stay alive before it is considered stable (resets early-drop counter).
early_drop_stable_window_seconds.title: Early Drop Stable Window Seconds

early_drop_max_retries.description: Maximum number of early-drop reconnect attempts before treating the failure as fatal.
early_drop_max_retries.title: Early Drop Max Retries

early_drop_backoff_initial_seconds.description: Initial backoff wait in seconds between early-drop reconnect attempts.
early_drop_backoff_initial_seconds.title: Early Drop Backoff Initial Seconds

early_drop_backoff_max_seconds.description: Maximum backoff wait in seconds between early-drop reconnect attempts.
early_drop_backoff_max_seconds.title: Early Drop Backoff Max Seconds

max_recovery_seconds.description: Maximum total wall-clock seconds to spend on all WebSocket recovery attempts before giving up.
max_recovery_seconds.title: Max Recovery Seconds

WebSocketConfig.title: WebSocketConfig
